body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.49-2 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @abbagail **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774536236610279) ## Action Performed: On Classic: 1. Go to Settings > Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Members 2. Click Invite 3. Observe that the personal message retains formatting from a previous invite On New: 1. Go to Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Invite 2. Enter a new email address 3. Select user role and proceed 4. Navigate to invite message settings 5. Observe a generic invite message (previous message not retained) 6. Edit the message (add links/formatting) 7. Send invite Switch back to Classic: 1. Go to Settings > Workspace > [Workspace Name] > Members 2. Click Invite 3. Observe that the personal message now displays raw HTML formatting 4. Send invite → formatting is not rendered correctly ## Expected Result: Invite messages should sync between New and Classic platforms Formatting (links, styling, etc.) should render consistently across both platforms Messages saved in one platform should appear correctly formatted in the other ## Actual Result: New platform does not load previously saved invite messages Messages edited in New appear as raw HTML in Classic Invite emails sent from Classic do not render formatting properly ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87cd1f0a-c98e-41c2-8097-369895d908b3 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @joekaufmanexpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774532029407719) ## Action Performed: 1. Create a pending Expensify Card transaction and place it on its own report. 2. Go to OldDot and verify you see the submit button. 3. Try to submit and verify you see an error saying the report can't be submitted because of the pending transaction. 4. Go to the report in NewDot. ## Expected Result: We show a submit button with the report in NewDot. Clicking it should throw a similar center-state (web)/bottom drawer (mobile) modal error explaining why it can't be submitted. The next step at the top of the report should also explain that the report can't be submitted until all pending transactions post. ## Proposed copy: Next Step: >All transactions are pending. You can’t submit this report until they post in a few days. Error Modal: >**Unable to submit report** >You can't submit this report because all the transactions are pending. They may take a few days to post. ## Actual Result: We hide the submit button in NewDot if a report only has pending Expensify Card transactions and the report next steps make no mention of why the report can't be submitted. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774532029407719) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.49-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/folder/13176668/test-cases/93408141 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** gloginme3+1@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** MacOS Monterey 12.7.4 / Chrome **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: Preconditions: 1. If tester is not from US, use US VPN. 2. Test on production. 3. Open an incognito window 4. Navigate to new.expensify.com (NewDot) (Very important) 5. Open console > Application > Cookies 6. Set the following cookies (check video for instructions) lc-qa = 1, lc-debug=1 7. Refresh the page NOTE: To bypass these conditions (account with expired free trial ) during QA, also set the lc-qa-mocked=1 cookie. This will ignore country and free trial checks STR: 1. Sign in with an account with expired free trial and control workspace 2. Navigate to Workspace settings and add a member (Should have access to the account) 3. Navigate to Reports > Insights 4. Open every tab: "Spend over time", "Top categories", "Top merchants", and "Top spenders" pages ## Expected Result: The banner: "Earn up to 2% cash back on all US purchases" and CTA: "Get started" and "X" to close the banner is present on all Insights tabs. ## Actual Result: The banner: "Earn up to 2% cash back on all US purchases" and CTA: "Get started" and "X" to close the banner is present on 'Top spenders' tab ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d876b1f4-b290-4f52-84d5-7a30ead66091 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com/ 2. Login to a new account with no DOB set. 3. Go to Profile > Private 4. Tap the date of birth field, which should open the date picker. ## Expected Result: The date selector is defaulted to whatever year/month is the youngest age we can accept (whatever year/month makes someone 18 years old now). It also shouldn't be possible to select a DOB that makes someone younger than 18 years old. This is how it works in other DOB forms, see below. ## Actual Result: The date picker defaults to today's date and allows you to select an invalid DOB younger than 18 years old. ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f99f9d55-521a-462c-9161-68c71e85d8ca https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d79e259-520b-4f9a-878d-5c2cbd6fa291 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] [Bug] Conflicting date filters when clicking chart data point

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Melvin Triage
labels.description: Automatic AI Analysis of an issue

body:
## Description When clicking on a data point from a chart view, the resulting view contains conflicting date filters — both a broad `date:last-12-months` filter and a specific date range filter (e.g., `date>=2025-11-01 date<=2025-11-30`) are applied simultaneously. ## Expected Behavior When clicking a specific data point (e.g., a single month), only the specific date range filter for that data point should be applied. The broader `date:last-12-months` filter should be removed or replaced. ## Current Behavior Both `date:last-12-months` AND the specific date range (e.g., `date>=2025-11-01 date<=2025-11-30`) are present as filters, which is contradictory. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the Home screen 2. View the insights chart (which defaults to last 12 months) 3. Click on a specific month's data point in the chart 4. Observe the filters applied to the resulting table view ## Platform All platforms
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @shubham1206agra

Launch app 2. Open the card expense 3. Tap more - split by date [ select random from and to dates more than 2 so that you can delete an expense] 4. Create a manual expense 11. Repeat steps 3 and step 4 12. Tap save creates the split expense ## Expected Result: Card expense: Verify shows total amount less than original amount error is shown Warning changes to error in red, user cannot save and shouldn't be able to. Cash expense: Error should show grey for cash expenses, and shouldn't ever turn red. Verify that ”Total amount is less than the original expense” is shown ## Actual Result: No error show when deleting a split expense by date from cash and card expense ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c8bcb24e-46e0-438a-85f0-9e7e1cce7258 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.47.0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/86048 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** ibellicotest+2250@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Motorola MotoG60 / Android 12 (Hybrid app) - Windows 11 / Chrome **App Component:** Search ## Action Performed: Prerequisite: Account has at least one workspace. 1. Open the Expensify app. 2. Open any workspace chat. 3. Create a manual expense. 4. Navigate to "Reports" > "Expenses" 5. Open the just created expense. 6. Tap on "More" > "Duplicate" 7. Note that duplicate expense is displayed highlighted on report. ## Expected Result: When an expense is duplicated while on "Reports" page, no navigation or highlight animation should be displayed on new expense. ## Actual Result: After an expense is duplicated, the new expense is displayed highlighted. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30721b20-115b-4cc0-9c18-fa87a542c5f0 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab
